steps.title: Understand the condition for a solution
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has at least one solution if and only if the lines are not parallel. The lines can either be identical (infinitely many solutions) or intersecting (one solution).

steps.title: Test each equation to determine which could be in the system
steps.content:
Equation Ⅰ: $10.5x + 7.5y = 3$
Divide by 1.5 to check if it's a scalar multiple of the original: $7x + 5y = 2$. Yes, Ⅰ is the same line. ✓

Equation Ⅱ: $10.5x - 7.5y = 3$
Rewrite the original in slope form: $y = -\frac{7}{5}x + \frac{2}{5}$ (slope $-\frac{7}{5}$).
Rewrite Ⅱ: $y = \frac{7}{5}x - \frac{2}{5}$ (slope $\frac{7}{5}$).
Different slopes mean the lines intersect. ✓

Both equations allow the system to have at least one solution. That matches choice C — C is correct.
